official_functions.role: Construct the official Play clientbound mount_screen_open packet fixture
Write, decode, and observe the official mount_screen_open body
Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound mount_screen_open frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns mount_screen_open to packet id 0x29.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the mount_screen_open id to ClientboundMountScreenOpenPacket.
The official mount_screen_open body is container id, inventory columns VarInt, and entity id int.
The fixture uses primitive constructor values, so it does not require initialized Entity, inventory, screen, Level, registry contents, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded fields, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte counts are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove mount entity existence, inventory/menu semantics, screen behavior, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer mount entity existence, inventory/menu semantics, screen behavior, render readiness, or client-load completion.
